name: The Liver Healing Sound (Xv / Shh)
instructions: Sit comfortably with your hands resting on your knees.
Inhale deeply, imagining a vibrant green light filling your Liver (right side, under the ribs).
Exhale slowly while making the sound 'Shhhhh' (like telling someone to be quiet).
As you exhale, imagine expelling dark, cloudy, stagnant anger and frustration.
Repeat 6 to 9 times.
safety_note: Do not force the exhale; let it be a smooth, continuous release.

name: The Heart Healing Sound (He / Haaa)
instructions: Sit tall and bring your awareness to your chest.
Inhale deeply, visualizing a warm red light filling your Heart.
Exhale with the mouth wide open, making the sound 'Haaaa' (like breathing fog onto a mirror).
Imagine releasing impatience, arrogance, and manic heat.
Repeat 6 to 9 times.
safety_note: Keep the throat relaxed to avoid straining the vocal cords.

name: The Spleen Healing Sound (Hu / Whoo)
instructions: Place your hands gently over your stomach.
Inhale, drawing golden-yellow light into your Spleen and Stomach center.
Exhale through rounded lips, making the sound 'Huuuu' or 'Whooo'.
Visualize overthinking, worry, and brain fog dissolving into the earth.
Repeat 6 to 9 times.
safety_note: Excellent to practice right after eating to aid digestion.

name: The Lung Healing Sound (Si / Ssss)
instructions: Sit with an open chest, resting hands on your thighs.
Inhale, visualizing a pure white light filling your Lungs.
Exhale through your teeth, making a hissing 'Sssss' sound.
Release grief, sadness, and clinging with the breath.
Repeat 6 to 9 times.

name: The Kidney Healing Sound (Chui / Chooo)
instructions: Place your hands on your lower back (Mingmen area).
Inhale, drawing deep blue or black light into the Kidneys.
Exhale, forming your lips into an 'O' and making the sound 'Chooo' (like blowing out a candle).
Release deep-seated fear and shock, replacing it with deep stillness.
Repeat 6 to 9 times.
safety_note: Keep the lower back warm during this practice.

name: Microcosmic Orbit (Xiao Zhou Tian)
instructions: Sit with your spine straight and touch the tip of your tongue to the roof of your mouth.
Inhale and draw energy up the spine (Du Mai), from the tailbone to the crown of the head.
Exhale and guide the energy down the front centerline of your body (Ren Mai), back to the lower abdomen (Dan Tian).
Let the breath be natural, focusing only on the continuous circular loop of light.
Continue for 5 to 10 minutes.
safety_note: Do not force the energy. If you get a headache, bring your focus purely to your lower belly.

name: Pulling Down the Heavens
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Inhale, sweeping your arms out to the sides and up above your head, gathering the pure Qi of the sky.
Exhale, turning your palms to face down, and slowly press them down the front of your body.
Visualize drawing calm, cooling energy down from the head, washing through the organs, and grounding into the earth.
Repeat 9 times.
safety_note: A perfect practice before bed to lower cognitive load.

name: Yongquan (KD-1) Grounding Press
instructions: Sit and cross one ankle over your opposite knee.
Locate KD-1 (Yongquan / Gushing Spring) on the sole of the foot, in the depression at the upper third of the sole when toes are curled.
Use your thumb to apply firm, circular pressure to the point.
Breathe deeply into your lower belly while massaging for 2-3 minutes per foot.
safety_note: Can be tender; adjust pressure to your comfort level.

name: Shenmen (HT-7) Spirit Gate Press
instructions: Look at your inner wrist. Locate HT-7 on the ulnar (pinky) side of the wrist crease, just inside the tendon.
Use your opposite thumb to gently press and massage the point in small circles.
Close your eyes and focus on slowing your heartbeat.
Massage for 1-2 minutes on each wrist.
safety_note: Use a gentle touch; the Heart meridian prefers soft persuasion.

name: Zusanli (ST-36) Vitality Tap
instructions: Sit with knees bent. Locate ST-36 about four finger-widths below the kneecap, one finger-width to the outside of the shinbone.
Using a loose fist, gently tap this point on both legs simultaneously.
Tap rhythmically for 1 to 2 minutes.
Feel the warmth and energy waking up your digestive center.
safety_note: Excellent for morning fatigue. Avoid tapping too hard.

name: Mo Fu (Abdominal Rubbing)
instructions: Lie on your back or sit comfortably.
Place your palms flat over your navel (one over the other).
Rub in slow, continuous circles, starting small and expanding outward.
Rub clockwise 36 times (to aid digestion and elimination).
Reverse and rub counter-clockwise 36 times (to gather and store Qi).
safety_note: Do not practice immediately after a very heavy meal; wait 30 minutes.

name: The Inner Smile
instructions: Close your eyes and generate a feeling of genuine gratitude or amusement, feeling a smile start behind your eyes.
Direct this smiling energy inward, letting it flow down like a waterfall.
Smile into your Heart, your Lungs, your Liver, your Kidneys, and your Spleen.
Acknowledge the hard work of your internal organs without judgment.
safety_note: If you cannot feel the smile, just fake the physical muscular smile; the body will follow.

name: Beating the Heavenly Drum (Ming Tian Gu)
instructions: Cover your ears firmly with your palms, with your fingers resting on the back of your head (base of the skull).
Place your index fingers over your middle fingers.
Snap the index fingers down forcefully onto the base of the skull.
You will hear a loud, resounding 'drum' sound in your head. Repeat 24 to 36 times.
safety_note: Do not practice if you have an active ear infection.

name: Water Up, Fire Down (Kan and Li)
instructions: Sit quietly. Visualize the hot, restless Fire of the Heart in your chest.
Visualize the cool, calm Water of the Kidneys in your lower back/abdomen.
With your breath, imagine drawing the cool Water up the spine to cool the Heart.
Imagine the warmth of the Heart Fire dropping down into the belly to warm the cold Water.
Continue breathing to circulate and balance these two poles.
safety_note: Keep your primary anchor of awareness in the lower belly (Dan Tian).

name: Hegu (LI-4) Release
instructions: Locate LI-4 on the back of the hand, at the highest point of the muscle when the thumb and index finger are brought together.
Use your opposite thumb to apply strong pressure angled slightly toward the bone of the index finger.
Massage in deep circles for 1-2 minutes per hand to release tension in the head and face.
safety_note: CONTRAINDICATED IN PREGNANCY. Do not use this point if pregnant.
